definition: UIP failure (Fama beta of -0.8 to -1.5) is a robust statistical anomaly (9/10 confidence), but the inference from this anomaly to an exploitable, compensated carry premium involves a logical gap (5/10 confidence on investability). The 55-60% directional hit rate says nothing about magnitude distribution — if appreciation episodes gain 2% and depreciation episodes lose 8%, expected return is negative despite positive hit rate. The full return distribution including the left tail was never presented.
evidence: The synthesis's own evidence (Sharpe decline, carry as leveraged equity beta, Peso problems) supports the critique internally
Full return distribution including left tail was never presented in the analysis
Strategies justified by 'UIP failure proves carry works' commit a logical error
open_questions: Whether the full return distribution including left tail produces positive expected value after transaction costs
Whether UIP failure is better explained by risk compensation, Peso problems, or market inefficiency
Whether the distinction between anomaly and premium is sufficiently recognized in institutional practice
